mission.title: Install and Configure Cozystack on Kubernetes
mission.description: Cozystack is a free PaaS platform that allows you to manage virtual machines, Kubernetes clusters, and databases seamlessly. Installing Cozystack enables you to build your own cloud infrastructure or provide a cost-effective development environment.

steps.title: Clone the Cozystack repository
steps.description:
Start by cloning the Cozystack GitHub repository to your local machine using the following command:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/cozystack/cozystack.git
cd cozystack
```

steps.title: Apply the Cozystack manifests
steps.description:
Use kubectl to apply the Cozystack manifests to your Kubernetes cluster. This will create the necessary resources:
```bash
kubectl apply -f k8s/namespace.yaml --create-namespace
kubectl apply -f k8s/deployment.yaml -n cozystack
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Cozystack pods are running correctly:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cozystack
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the Cozystack deployment to ensure optimal performance:
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment/cozystack --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi -n cozystack
```

steps.title: Access the Cozystack API
steps.description:
Set up port forwarding to access the Cozystack API locally:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/cozystack 8080:80 -n cozystack
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Cozystack deployment
uninstall.description:
Delete the deployment and service resources:
```bash
kubectl delete -f k8s/deployment.yaml -n cozystack
```

uninstall.title: Clean up the namespace
uninstall.description:
Remove the Cozystack namespace to clean up all resources:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace cozystack
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and resources:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cozystack -o yaml > cozystack-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the repository
upgrade.description:
Pull the latest changes from the Cozystack repository:
```bash
git pull origin main
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Cozystack deployment
upgrade.description:
Reapply the updated manifests to upgrade Cozystack:
```bash
kubectl apply -f k8s/deployment.yaml -n cozystack
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Cozystack pods are running the new version:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cozystack
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods not starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the Cozystack pods are not starting, check the pod logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n cozystack
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Cozystack API, ensure the service is running:
```bash
kubectl get svc -n cozystack
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter resource allocation issues, check the resource limits and adjust them accordingly:
```bash
kubectl describe deployment cozystack -n cozystack
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Cozystack will have all pods running without errors, and you will be able to access the Cozystack API through the configured port. Resource limits should be set to ensure optimal performance.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f k8s/namespace.yaml --create-namespace
kubectl apply -f k8s/deployment.yaml -n cozystack

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and kubectl installed on your machine.
